description: World-class PostgreSQL primary + replica monitoring with connection pooling, WAL, replication lag, table bloat, vacuum stats, cache performance, lock analysis, and disk usage.

panels.title: Server Role
panels.description: Current server role (primary or standby)

panels.title: PostgreSQL Uptime
panels.description: Time since the postgres-exporter (and its sidecar Postgres pod) started. Proxy for Postgres uptime.

panels.title: Commit Ratio
panels.description: Percentage of transactions that commit vs rollback

panels.title: Deadlocks & Conflicts / s
panels.description: Rate of deadlocks and query conflicts

panels.title: Replication Slots & Replicas
panels.description: Number of replication slots and active replicas

panels.title: Connection State Distribution
panels.description: Connections grouped by state (active, idle, idle in transaction, idle in transaction (aborted), fastpath function call, disabled)

panels.title: Active Connections vs Max
panels.description: Current active connections and max configured

panels.title: Connection Pool Utilization
panels.description: Percentage of max connections in use

panels.title: Connections Per Database
panels.description: Active connections broken down by database

panels.title: Transaction Rate (Commits & Rollbacks)
panels.description: Per-database commit and rollback rates

panels.title: Tuple Operations Rate
panels.description: Insert, update, delete, fetch rates per database

panels.title: Row Fetch (SELECT) Rate
panels.description: Rows returned per second by SELECT queries

panels.title: Shared Buffer Cache Hit Ratio
panels.description: Should be > 0.95 for production. Low ratio indicates need for more shared_buffers or better queries.

panels.title: Buffer Hit & Read Throughput
panels.description: Shared buffer I/O in bytes/second. Hit throughput should far exceed disk reads.

panels.title: Temp File Write Rate
panels.description: Bytes written to temp files (disk-backed sort/hashes). Non-zero means queries exceed work_mem.

panels.title: Temp File Count Rate
panels.description: Number of temporary files created per second

panels.title: Replication Lag (Bytes)
panels.description: Bytes of WAL data not yet applied on each replica. Red line at ~10MB indicates high lag.

panels.title: Replication Lag (Time - via pg_stat_replication)
panels.description: Seconds since each replica last reported back (time() - pg_stat_replication_reply_time).

panels.title: Replication Network Traffic
panels.description: WAL data sent/received per replica

panels.title: Replication WAL Byte Throughput
panels.description: Rate of WAL bytes sent to each replica (rate over 5m of pg_stat_replication_pg_current_wal_lsn_bytes).

panels.title: Replication Lag (Seconds) - PRIMARY ONLY
panels.description: Replication lag in seconds for each replica. Green < 2s, Yellow 2-10s, Red > 10s. Monitor from primary.

panels.title: WAL Generation Rate
panels.description: Bytes of WAL generated per second

panels.title: WAL Files Count
panels.description: Number of WAL files. Standard WAL file size is 16MB.

panels.title: Background Writer Checkpoint Write
panels.description: Checkpoint write throughput. High values may indicate heavy checkpoint activity.

panels.title: Database Sizes
panels.description: Size of each database in bytes

panels.title: Tuple Churn (Live vs Dead, per DB)
panels.description: Per-database tuple-level write activity as a proxy for live/dead churn (pg_stat_user_tables not exported). Live = insert+update rate, Dead = delete rate.

panels.title: Dead Tuple Rate by Database
panels.description: Per-database delete rate as a proxy for dead-tuple creation (table-level pg_stat_user_tables metrics are not exported by this postgres-exporter).

panels.title: SELECT Efficiency (Returned vs Fetched, per DB)
panels.description: Rows returned (scanned) vs rows fetched (kept) per 5m. Large gaps indicate potential missing indexes / seq-scans. Table-level pg_stat_user_tables metrics are not exported by this postgres-exporter.

panels.title: Lock Count by Mode
panels.description: Current lock counts by mode (AccessShare, RowShare, Exclusive, etc.). High Exclusive counts indicate blocking.

panels.title: Lock Status (by Database & Mode)
panels.description: Per-database lock counts by lock mode. postgres-exporter's pg_locks_count does not expose a `granted` label, so waiting-vs-granted cannot be distinguished here; mode=accessexclusivelock is the main blocker to watch.

panels.title: Lock Summary (Nonzero)
panels.description: Active (nonzero) lock counts grouped by database and mode. `lock_type` / `granted` labels are not exported by postgres-exporter, so summarisation is limited to (datname, mode).

panels.title: Deadlocks & Conflicts Rate
panels.description: Rate of deadlocks and query conflicts (vacuum conflicts, lock conflicts, buffer pin)

panels.title: Buffer Writes by Type
panels.description: Bytes written per second by different bgwriter mechanisms. Allocated = total buffer writes.

panels.title: Checkpoint Rate & Sync Time
panels.description: Checkpoint request rate and sync time. High sync time indicates slow disk I/O.

panels.title: Background Writer Metrics
panels.description: Buffer write counts, checksum operations, and maxwritten stops

panels.title: PostgreSQL Runtime Summary
panels.description: Runtime summary: average active connections, total DB size (MB), and WAL storage (MB). Configured postgres settings (shared_buffers, work_mem, max_connections) are not exported by this postgres-exporter.

title: PostgreSQL - Primary & Replica Overview
